spec_match.rationale: The page now demonstrates a named view timeline on the article content and a sidebar meter using `animation-range: scroll 0% scroll 100%`.

interactive.rationale: Every chapter button, top/middle/bottom control, active-state readout, and inactive-timeline check is interactive and updates the page state.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The feature index now describes the real pairing of JavaScript chapter navigation with a CSS named view-timeline progress meter.

edge_cases.rationale: The inactive-timeline check constructs a hidden ViewTimeline subject and reports the null-current-time branch instead of hiding unsupported behavior.

references.rationale: The page now includes the required ChromeStatus link for feature 6522328437620736.

design_system.rationale: The page no longer has source inline handlers or source inline styles, and the touched UI uses design-system variables with paired button colors.

summary: Verified with chrome-devtools-mcp at `/v147/timeline-named-range-scroll/chapter-navigator/?verify=after-timeline-final`: all five chapter buttons and top/middle/bottom controls produced stable active states, the hidden-subject inactive ViewTimeline branch stayed visible, the ChromeStatus link is present, and the console has no warnings or errors.
